text: ‘For love of him’; or perhaps, ‘in his love’.
Some Greek manuscripts omit the words ‘of the love’.

It is not certain whether this means that we are God’s inheritance (cf. Deut. 32.9) or that God gives us an inheritance, as in verse 14 above. Ifour inheritance is meant, ‘redemption’ is perhaps best understood as implying that our inheritance is redeemed for us (cf. Lev. 25.25).
The Greek here has ‘to be the head of the Church above everything else’, the Latin, ‘to be head over the whole Church’.
‘Who … is complete’; the Greek verb used here may mean this, or it may mean ‘who completes all things in all men (or in all things)’.
